title: marsilea hirsuta var. exarata
intro: Marsilea hirsuta var. exarata, also known as the hairy waterclover, is a plant species that belongs to the family Marsileaceae. It is a type of aquatic plant that is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Traditionally, Marsilea hirsuta var. exarata has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for its health benefits.
